data.description: There should be no size selector on the main page, nor error container. When a user clicks Buy A Shirt, they should be redirected to a new page that has checkout info and a size selector
data.title: Buy a shirt should be a seprate page

data.body:
Current state (post-TYB-12): the generator page has text inputs, size picker (S/M/L/XL radios, default M), Buy button, and an inline error container. Clicking Buy POSTs {text, middletext, variant_id} to /api/checkout/start, the server creates the Printful sync_product + Stripe session, and returns checkout_url which the browser redirects to.

Requested flow:
1. Generator page (`/`) — text inputs + Buy button only. Size picker and #buy-error container removed.
2. Click Buy → navigate to a new checkout page carrying the design via query params (text, middletext).
3. New checkout page — design preview + price + size selector + final Buy button. Final Buy POSTs to /api/checkout/start and redirects to Stripe.

Key planning decisions to flag:
- Separate HTML file (e.g., checkout.html) vs single-page view swap. Real separate file matches 'redirected to a new page' literally and gives the browser a proper history entry/title; recommended.
- Design preview on checkout page: re-render the SVG client-side from query params (cheap, no server round-trip) or fetch from /api/render (extra latency). Client-side recommended.
- Where does the price come from on the client? Hardcoded $30 vs fetched from server. Hardcoded is fine for V1 — the server is still the source of truth for the Stripe Session price.
- Back button: clicking back from checkout to generator should restore text inputs (URL has the params; existing init() handles this).
- Worldwide free shipping copy on checkout page (per TYB-12 decision).

Out of scope: the thanks state and canceled banner. Those are reached via Stripe redirect, unchanged.

Implementation complete. Two commits:
- 39aa24c TYB-23: feat(public): split buy flow into generator + /checkout page
- 5c369df TYB-23: feat(httpserver): route GET /checkout to checkout.html

Changes per plan:
- server/public/index.html: removed #size-picker, #buy-error, #variant-catalog script+comment. Kept inputs, SVG, image-view overlay, Save Image, Buy.
- server/public/checkout.html: new file. Back link, SVG block (copied verbatim from index.html), $30 USD, free worldwide shipping copy, S/M/L/XL radios (default M), #buy-shirt-final, hidden #buy-error, variant-catalog JSON. Includes the price-is-informational and keep-in-sync-with-catalog.go comments.
- server/public/script.js: createTShirt now navigates to /checkout?text=...&middletext=... via URLSearchParams. Fetch+error logic moved to new startCheckout() function gated on #buy-shirt-final. New populatePreview() reads ?text/?middletext and drives the SVG on /checkout. window.onload branches: init() + editor keyup handlers only on /, populatePreview() only on /checkout. popstate, renderThanksState, renderCanceledState, createImage unchanged.
- server/public/style.css: added body.checkout-page (overflow:auto override), .back-link, .checkout-preview, .checkout-details, .checkout-price, .checkout-shipping.
- server/internal/httpserver/router.go: added mux.HandleFunc('GET /checkout', ...) using http.ServeFileFS over the existing publicFS. Registered before the '/' fall-through.
- server/public/embed.go: no change — existing *.html glob already covers checkout.html.

Deviations from plan: none of substance. One minor thing — moved the 'export' button click listener inside the non-checkout branch since the export button is generator-only; the original code attached it unconditionally and would have NPE'd on /checkout where there is no #export. Wrapped in an if-check for safety.

Verification:
- cd server && go build ./... — OK
- go test -count=1 ./... — all green (cmd/server, httpserver, printful, render, stripe)
- go vet ./... — OK
- Started DATA_DIR=/tmp/tyb23-impl PORT=8189 PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8189 binary; curl smoke:
  - GET / → 200, contains id="main-input"; no size-picker / no buy-error / no variant-catalog (all 0 matches)
  - GET /checkout → 200, contains buy-shirt-final, size-picker, variant-catalog, <svg, and the $30 string (2 matches)
  - GET /script.js → 200, contains 'function populatePreview', 'function startCheckout', and "window.location.href = '/checkout"
  - GET /checkout?text=HELLO&middletext=WORLD → 200, contains #filled-text and .back-link (population happens client-side at runtime — server returns the template).

Caveat: manual browser test not run — needs human verification of the actual UX flow (typing on /, clicking Buy, seeing /checkout with the rendered SVG, clicking Buy there to hit Stripe redirect, Back button restoring inputs). The curl smokes only confirm static structure, not the runtime SVG population, history pushState behavior, or the Stripe redirect.

**Verdict: PASS**

**What I reviewed:** plan, both commits (39aa24c + 5c369df), current state of index.html, checkout.html, script.js, style.css, router.go, embed.go. Built (`go build ./cmd/server`), tested (`go test ./...`), vetted (`go vet ./...`) — all clean. Ran the server on :8190 and curl-smoked /, /checkout, /script.js, and /api/checkout/start.

**Acceptance criteria walkthrough:**

1. `/` clean of `#size-picker`, `#buy-error`, `#variant-catalog` — verified by grep + curl. PASS.
2. `#buy-shirt` on `/` performs `window.location.href = '/checkout?...'` — script.js:278, no fetch in createTShirt. PASS.
3. `/checkout` served 200 — `curl -I /checkout` returns `200 text/html; charset=utf-8`. PASS.
4. SVG preview re-rendered from query params — `populatePreview()` script.js:149 reads `?text`/`?middletext`, drives `.main-text` / `#filled-text`, calls `resizeSVG`. Checkout HTML has full 7-tspan SVG block (matches index.html count). PASS.
5. `/checkout` shows $30 USD, "Free worldwide shipping", S/M/L/XL radios (M default), Buy button, hidden `#buy-error` — all four confirmed via curl + grep. PASS.
6. Final Buy POSTs `{text, middletext, variant_id}` to `/api/checkout/start` and redirects on success — script.js:298–342. PASS.
7. 400/502/503 errors render inline — `startCheckout` retains the full error mapping (script.js:306–340). I curled the endpoint with variant_id:0 and got `400 validation_failed/variant_id is required`; the client maps that to `data.message` in `#buy-error`. PASS.
8. Browser Back from `/checkout` returns to `/` — implemented as plain `window.location.href = '/checkout...'`, so history is browser-native; no `history.pushState` in `createTShirt`. PASS.
9. `?canceled=1` and `?session_id` branches unchanged — script.js:10–18, top-of-onload, runs on both pages but no-ops without the query. PASS.
10. `go build` + `go test` pass. PASS.

**Hotspot findings:**

- `#export` deviation (script.js:85–86): listener moved inside the else (non-checkout) branch and uses `if (exportBtn)` for null-safety. Avoids NPE on /checkout, Save Image still works on /. Better than the plan's spec. ACCEPT.
- Router order (router.go:49 then :56): `mux.HandleFunc("GET /checkout", ...)` registered before `mux.Handle("/", staticHandler)`. Go 1.22 method-prefixed patterns are more specific so it would work either way, but ordering matches the plan. PASS.
- `http.ServeFileFS` over `publicFS` (router.go:50): `publicFS := public.FS()` on router.go:18 is in scope; resolves to `checkout.html` in the embedded FS. PASS.
- Embed (embed.go:21): `*.html` glob unchanged — `checkout.html` lands in the binary; `curl /checkout` proves it. PASS.
- Variant-catalog placeholder zeros: `readSelectedVariantID()` returns 0 when catalog values are 0; server returns 400 `validation_failed`; `startCheckout` 400 branch surfaces `data.message` in `#buy-error`. Graceful — not a crash, not a silent zero to Stripe. PASS.
- popstate handler still wires on both pages (script.js:94–101); harmless on /checkout since `#image-view` doesn't exist there and the `if (view && !view.hidden ...)` guard short-circuits. PASS.

**Convention checks:**
- Both commits prefixed `TYB-23:`. PASS.
- No emojis in the diff. PASS.
- No `--no-verify`. PASS.
- Comments explain WHY (route choice, font duplication rationale, why init() can't run on /checkout, why placeholder zeros surface as errors). PASS.
- No scope creep — thanks/canceled stayed on /, SVG template unchanged, no test framework introduced.

**Doc check:** This task ships user-facing routes (new /checkout URL) and a new HTML asset, but no env vars/commands/config knobs and no load-bearing architectural decisions that the plan file doesn't already capture for posterity. The README's "Routes" section (if present) might benefit from listing /checkout — checked: README does not enumerate per-page URLs (it documents API endpoints + env vars only). No README update required by the documentation policy as written.

**Verdict:** PASS. Moving to done.

**Human-only verification (cannot be automated from here):**
- Real browser: type HELLO/WORLD on /, click Buy, confirm /checkout?text=HELLO&middletext=WORLD shows HELLO/WORLD in the SVG, $30, shipping copy, M-checked, Buy button.
- Browser Back from /checkout lands on /?text=HELLO&middletext=WORLD with inputs prefilled.
- With real Printful + Stripe creds and a populated variant catalog (non-zero ids), final Buy redirects to a Stripe Checkout page.
- Save Image still works on / (the #export deviation is the only behavior change near it).
